IUtilities java.lang.Object jni.JNIUtilities public class JNIUtilities extends java.lang.Object A collection of utilities for generating Java Native Interface (JNI) classes. For information about JNI, see http://java.sun.com/docs/books/tutorial/nativ

see http://java.sun.com/docs/books/tutorial/native1.1/concepts/index.html For information about how to create shared libraries under Windows using Cygwin, see http://cygwin.com/cygwin-ug-net/dll.html For information about using JNI with Cygwin, s

tml For information about using JNI with Cygwin, see http://www.inonit.com/cygwin/jni/helloWorld/c.html How to call an actor that calls C code that returns a double In this example, we call a C method called meaningOfLife() that returns a double. Cre

called meaningOfLife() that returns a double. Create the C file called meaningOfLife.c that contains // Return the answer to "the meaning of life, the universe, and everything" double meaningOfLife() { return 42.0; } Create meaningOfLife.h

e() { return 42.0; } Create meaningOfLife.h that contains extern "C" double meaningOfLife(); Compile and create the shared library. Under Windows, we create a .dll gcc -shared -o meaningOfLife.dll meaningOfLife.c Set the CLASSPATH to includ

fe.dll meaningOfLife.c Set the CLASSPATH to include the current directory CLASSPATH=. export CLASSPATH Start up Vergil with the JNI interface enabled. $PTII/bin/vergil -jni Note that the -jni option may go away in the future if we merge the jni

n may go away in the future if we merge the jni facility into the main tree Create a new model with File -> New -> Graph Editor Drag in the JNIActor from the jni folder Right click on the actor and select Configure Arguments. Fill in the form as f

select Configure Arguments. Fill in the form as follows Name: output C or C++ type: double Kind: return Select Ok and then Commit to close the argument configurer Right Click on the actor to edit the parameters libraryDirectory "" nativeFuncti

the parameters libraryDirectory "" nativeFunction: "meaningOfLife" nativeLibrary "meaningOfLife" Select Commit to close the Edit Parameters window Select JNI from the menu and then select Generate C Interface FIXME: Copy the dlls into a directo

te C Interface FIXME: Copy the dlls into a directory that is in the path cp meaningOfLife.dll $PTII/bin cp jni/jnimeaningOfLife/JnijnimeaningOfLife.dll $PTII/bin Save the model. FIXME: Because of an apparent bug, it is necessary to save the mod

n apparent bug, it is necessary to save the model for the port we just created to appear Add a Display actor from the Sink folder in the Actor Library and connect the input of the Display actor to the output of the meaningOfLife Actor FIXME:

to the output of the meaningOfLife Actor FIXME: Because of a bug in the Ptolemy interface to the JNI actor, you must specify the type of the output of the meaningOfLife actor by right clicking on the actor and selecting Configure Ports an

g on the actor and selecting Configure Ports and then entering double for output type Drag in a SDF director and set the number of iterations to 1. Select Run The answer to "the meaning of life, the universe, and everything" should appear in

the universe, and everything" should appear in the Display actor. If you get an error like: /cygdrive/c/Program Files/j2sdk1.4.1_01/include/win32/jni_md.h:16: syntax error before `;' Then see http://www.xraylith.wisc.edu/~khan/software/gnu-win32/

://www.xraylith.wisc.edu/~khan/software/gnu-win32/README.jni.txt You might need to edit jni_md.h, the above URL says GCC doesn't have a __int64 built-in, and this patch basically uses "long long" instead. Edit the file /include/win32/jni_md.h, Whe

nstead. Edit the file /include/win32/jni_md.h, Where is the installation root (eg., c:/jdk1.1.7A). Replace the segment: typedef long jint; typedef __int64 jlong; with: typedef long jint; #ifdef __GNUC__ typedef long long jlong; #else t

__GNUC__ typedef long long jlong; #else typedef __int64 jlong; #endif typedef signed char jbyte; Since: Ptolemy II 2.3 Version: $Id: JNIUtilities.java,v 1.49 2004/04/21 03:04:25 cxh Exp $ Author: Vincent Arnould (vincent.arnould@thales
